prior_hypotheses:
- Bidirectional Thalamocortical Coupling and Structural Morphology in Insomnia: This hypothesis proposes a bidirectional interaction between thalamic structural morphology and thalamocortical coupling in insomnia, integrating multimodal neuroimaging data.
- Thalamic Morphology Modulates Thalamocortical Coupling in Insomnia: This hypothesis proposes that thalamic morphology influences thalamocortical coupling in insomnia, linking structural and functional brain changes.
- Bidirectional Thalamocortical Coupling and Structural Morphology with Cross-Pollinated Mechanisms in Insomnia: This hypothesis integrates bidirectional thalamocortical coupling and structural morphology with cross-pollinated mechanisms from other top hypotheses, enhancing coherence and evidence grounding.
- Thalamocortical Coupling Modulates Insomnia Severity via Structural Morphology: This hypothesis proposes that thalamocortical coupling influences insomnia severity through structural morphology changes, integrating functional and structural neuroimaging data.
- Thalamic Morphology Modulates Thalamocortical Coupling in Insomnia: This hypothesis proposes that thalamic morphology influences thalamocortical coupling in insomnia, linking structural and functional brain changes.
